# From Loop to RSI · Sovereign Invention Governance v50

`/loop-to-rsi.html` is a browser-local public demonstration of how a restartable agent loop becomes deterministic RSI governance.

## What it demonstrates

A long-running loop is useful when it can restart, read traces, write state, and expose bottlenecks. RSI adds the governance control plane: TARGET → EMIT → FILTER → ATLAS → TEST-PLAN → EVAL → INSERT → PROMOTE.

The page demonstrates:

- contract-first loop operation;
- separated roles and stage authority;
- append-only RSI state;
- Evidence Contact Index discipline;
- baseline-comparative evaluation;
- high-novelty / Move-37 handling;
- shock-test persistence;
- dossier packaging;
- human authority boundary.

## Public-safe boundary

The page does not collect user data, connect a wallet, approve tokens, switch networks, broadcast transactions, move funds, call external services, or grant production authority. It is an educational local control-plane simulation.

## Claim boundary

The page does not claim achieved RSI, AGI, ASI, empirical SOTA, external audit completion, production certification, autonomous sovereignty, or production authority. Stronger claims require real tasks, baselines, executed evidence, replayable ProofBundles, cost/risk ledgers, validator reports, delayed outcomes, and independent review.

## Export

The page exports a public-safe `GoalOS.RSIDossier` JSON containing local state, gates, novelty, ECI, replay, persistence, and public-safe boundaries.
